name: Frequency Count (Greedy)
summary: Count every letter, then take every pair you can. Pairs mirror around the centre; at most one leftover odd letter can sit in the middle.
visualizationIntent: init: show the string laid out and an empty frequency map. update: for each character, fly it into its bucket and increment the tally. loop: walk the string left to right, then walk the collected keys. check: test whether a letter's count is odd. return: show the final palindrome length, including the +1 centre if an odd letter existed.

lineExplanations.3: Create an empty tally — one entry per distinct letter.
lineExplanations.4: Start scanning the string at index 0.
lineExplanations.5: Holds the character being counted right now.
lineExplanations.7: Read the character at the current index.
lineExplanations.8: Have we seen this letter before?
lineExplanations.9: Yes — bump its tally by one.
lineExplanations.11: No — this is its first sighting, so start its tally at 1.
lineExplanations.13: The running palindrome length, built from pairs.
lineExplanations.14: Tracks whether ANY letter had an odd count.
lineExplanations.15: Collect the distinct letters so we can walk them.
lineExplanations.16: Start walking the distinct letters at index 0.
lineExplanations.17: Clear the scan character — the first pass is over.
lineExplanations.18: Holds the tally of the letter being folded in.
lineExplanations.19: Keep going while distinct letters remain.
lineExplanations.21: Look up how many times it appeared.
lineExplanations.22: Add only the PAIRS: count // 2 pairs, each worth 2 characters.
lineExplanations.23: Did this letter have a leftover (odd count)?
lineExplanations.24: Remember that a spare letter exists — it can sit at the centre.
lineExplanations.25: Move to the next distinct letter.
lineExplanations.26: Was there at least one leftover letter?
lineExplanations.27: Add 1 for the single centre character a palindrome allows.
lineExplanations.28: Return the longest palindrome length we can build.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: is the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def longestPalindrome(self, s): defines the method; s is the input string.
syntaxExplanations.3: {} creates an empty dictionary — a letter → count tally.
syntaxExplanations.4: i = 0 starts the scan index at the first character.
syntaxExplanations.5: ch = "" declares the variable up front so it always exists.
syntaxExplanations.6: len(s) is the string's length; the loop runs while i is still inside it.
syntaxExplanations.7: s[i] reads a single character by index, just like a list.
syntaxExplanations.8: 'ch in counts' is True when the dictionary already has that key.
syntaxExplanations.9: counts[ch] + 1 reads the old tally and stores one more.
syntaxExplanations.10: else: covers the case where the key is not present yet.
syntaxExplanations.11: counts[ch] = 1 creates the key with an initial tally of 1.
syntaxExplanations.12: i = i + 1 advances to the next character.
syntaxExplanations.13: length = 0 starts the answer at zero.
syntaxExplanations.14: has_odd = False is a boolean flag, set later if needed.
syntaxExplanations.15: counts.keys() gives the distinct letters; list(...) makes them indexable.
syntaxExplanations.16: ki = 0 is a separate index for walking the keys.
syntaxExplanations.17: ch = "" resets the scan character so it no longer highlights a bucket.
syntaxExplanations.18: count = 0 declares the tally variable used in the second pass.
syntaxExplanations.19: while ki < len(keys): walks every distinct letter once.
syntaxExplanations.20: keys[ki] reads the ki-th distinct letter.
syntaxExplanations.21: counts[key] looks that letter's tally up in the dictionary.
syntaxExplanations.22: count // 2 is integer division (pairs); * 2 turns pairs back into characters.
syntaxExplanations.23: count % 2 is the remainder after dividing by 2 — 1 means odd.
syntaxExplanations.24: has_odd = True records that a spare character exists.
syntaxExplanations.25: ki = ki + 1 advances to the next distinct letter.
syntaxExplanations.26: if has_odd: is True when any letter had a leftover.
syntaxExplanations.27: length = length + 1 adds the single centre character.
syntaxExplanations.28: return length hands back the final answer.
